title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Giles leads Mr. Palazzini to the living room, while Molly experiences a moment of hysterical laughter and distress after Palazzini hums "Three Blind Mice" and discusses the song's cruel lyrics. Jules attempts to calm Molly, noting that murder investigations are more pressing than cooking. In the hallway, they encounter Christopher Lane before entering the living room, where Inspector Thrope and Captain Metcalf are waiting.

Chief Constable Trotter addresses the group, noting that Mrs. Boyle is dead and will not eat again. He reveals that he has gathered everyone to conduct an experiment. Trotter explains that while he cannot verify the previous statements regarding everyone's locations at the time of the murder, he knows that four statements are true and one is false. He intends to identify the liar to find the murderer by having the individuals reenact their behaviors from the time of the crime.

Although Colonel Metcalf scoffs at the idea and Christopher Lane expresses confusion, Jules agrees to cooperate. Trotter begins the process by asking Palazzini to demonstrate his previous action of playing the piano. Palazzini plays "Three Blind Mice" on the grand piano. Trotter then instructs Molly to sit at the piano to repeat the action. He assigns specific tasks to the others to validate their statements: Lane goes to the kitchen to assist Molly, Palazzini goes to Lane's bedroom to play "Three Blind Mice," Metcalf goes to Mr. Davis's bedroom to check the phone, and Mr. Davis goes to the hall and basement. As the group departs, Palazzini remarks on the police's enjoyment of indoor games.
